title: Supervised Learning
question: What does supervised learning require?
explanation: Supervised learning maps inputs to known outputs using labeled data. Encryption, DNS, and no-data training are unrelated.
referenceNote: Curated professional MCQ. Each option is designed to test a specific interview concept.

title: Train/Test Split
question: Why do we use a test set?
options: To estimate performance on unseen data
To train longer on the same examples
To convert labels into DNS records
correctAnswer: To estimate performance on unseen data
explanation: A test set approximates generalization. It should not be used for training, does not remove all features, and is unrelated to DNS.
sourceLine: Why do we use a test set?
referenceNote: Curated professional MCQ. Each option is designed to test a specific interview concept.

title: Overfitting
question: What is overfitting?
options: Model learns training noise and fails to generalize well
correctAnswer: Model learns training noise and fails to generalize well
explanation: Overfitting means training performance is high but unseen performance suffers. It is not about having no parameters, dataset size alone, or networking.
referenceNote: Curated professional MCQ. Each option is designed to test a specific interview concept.

title: Precision vs Recall
question: In a spam classifier, what does high recall mean?
options: Most actual spam emails are detected
Every predicted spam is always correct
correctAnswer: Most actual spam emails are detected
explanation: Recall measures how many actual positives are found. Precision measures how many predicted positives are correct. The other options are unrelated.
sourceLine: In a spam classifier, what does high recall mean?
referenceNote: Curated professional MCQ. Each option is designed to test a specific interview concept.

title: RAG Purpose
question: Why is Retrieval-Augmented Generation used with LLMs?
options: To provide relevant external context before generating an answer
To guarantee every answer is always correct
correctAnswer: To provide relevant external context before generating an answer
explanation: RAG retrieves useful context and passes it to the model. It does not delete weights, avoid documents, or guarantee correctness.
sourceLine: Why is Retrieval-Augmented Generation used with LLMs?
referenceNote: Curated professional MCQ. Each option is designed to test a specific interview concept.

title: Embeddings
question: What does an embedding represent?
options: Data encoded as a vector capturing semantic similarity
correctAnswer: Data encoded as a vector capturing semantic similarity
explanation: Embeddings place similar items near each other in vector space. They are not passwords, scheduler values, or certificates.
referenceNote: Curated professional MCQ. Each option is designed to test a specific interview concept.

title: Gradient Descent
question: What does gradient descent do during model training?
options: Updates parameters in a direction that reduces the loss
correctAnswer: Updates parameters in a direction that reduces the loss
explanation: Gradient descent uses gradients of the loss to update parameters. It is unrelated to packets, indexes, or encryption.
sourceLine: What does gradient descent do during model training?
referenceNote: Curated professional MCQ. Each option is designed to test a specific interview concept.

title: Data Leakage
question: Why is data leakage dangerous?
options: It lets training use information that would not be available in real prediction
It removes the need for validation
correctAnswer: It lets training use information that would not be available in real prediction
explanation: Leakage inflates evaluation and fails in production. It does not improve real generalization, encrypt labels, or remove validation needs.
referenceNote: Curated professional MCQ. Each option is designed to test a specific interview concept.
